faq.title: Common Questions
faq.desc: Everything you need to know about the platform.
faq.q1: How do I report a civic issue?
faq.a1: Simply sign in to your citizen account, click 'Report Issue', pin the location on the map, and upload a photo with details.
faq.a2: Each department has specific SLAs. You'll receive real-time updates as the status changes from submitted to resolved.
faq.a3: Yes. Your privacy is our priority. Personal details are only visible to authorized department staff handling your case.
faq.q4: Can I track my complaint status?
faq.a4: Absolutely. Every report gets a unique ID (e.g., #FMC-1042) that you can track in real-time on your dashboard.
faq.a5: Currently, we cover all major metropolitan zones and surrounding districts. We are expanding to more areas every month.
faq.q6: How are complaints assigned to the right department?
faq.a6: When you submit a complaint, our AI classifier reads your title and description and instantly routes it to the correct department — Road, Water, Streetlight, Garbage, or Drainage — so the right team is notified immediately.
faq.q7: What is a ticket and how do I use it?
faq.a7: A ticket is a dedicated communication thread created when a staff member accepts your complaint. It allows you to chat directly with the person fixing your issue and track progress in real-time.
faq.q8: Who is the Head of Department?
faq.a8: Each department has a dedicated HOD who oversees all incoming complaints, assigns them to staff members, and monitors the overall department resolution rate.
faq.q9: How does the admin send announcements?
faq.a9: The system administrator can dispatch targeted announcements (broadcasts) to the entire city or specific roles to communicate platform updates or emergency alerts.

report.title: Report an Issue
report.desc: Provide details about the civic problem you've encountered.
report.mapPlaceholder: Click on map to select location
report.mapHint: Click anywhere on the map to set the precise location of the issue.
report.descriptionPlaceholder: Describe the issue in detail. Mention any specific hazards or urgency.
tips: Be specific about the location to help our team find it faster.
Clear photos help us assess the priority and required tools.
Mention if the issue is causing immediate danger.
report.termsHint: By submitting, you agree to our terms of service and confirm the information provided is accurate.

help.title: User Guide
help.subtitle: Learn how to use FixMyCity to improve your neighborhood.
help.reportDesc: To report a new issue, navigate to the "Report Issue" page from the sidebar. Fill in the title, select a relevant category, provide the location, and write a detailed description. Uploading a clear photo of the issue helps our team assess and prioritize the repair more effectively.
help.lifecycleSubtitle: Every reported issue goes through a structured process:

submitted.label: SUBMITTED
submitted.desc: Your report has been received and is waiting for initial review.

underReview.label: UNDER REVIEW
underReview.desc: An administrator is verifying the details and assessing priority.

assigned.label: ASSIGNED
assigned.desc: The issue has been routed to the appropriate city department.

inProgress.label: IN PROGRESS
inProgress.desc: A repair crew has been dispatched and work is underway.

resolved.label: RESOLVED
resolved.desc: The work is complete and the issue has been fixed.

rejected.label: REJECTED
rejected.desc: The report was invalid or falls outside city jurisdiction.

help.trackDesc: You can monitor all your reports in the "My Complaints" section. Each report shows a status badge and a priority level. Clicking on a specific complaint will show you a detailed timeline of all actions taken by city officials.
help.adminDesc: Administrators use the Admin Dashboard to oversee city-wide issues. They can filter reports by priority, assign them to specific departments (Road, Water, Electricity, etc.), and update the status as work progresses. This ensures that every citizen report is handled by the right team.
